rulings.Case Title: MEGAN KOSNOSKI VS. NO MORE DIRT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 1, 2018, Line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT FAIRMONT HOTEL MANAGEMENT, L.P.'s Motion For Protective Order And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Diana Kruze, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court GRANTS-IN-PART and DENIES-IN-PART Fairmont's motion for a protective order and request for sanctions. With respect to Cross-Defendant No More Dirt's ("NMD") deposition notices on counsel Posard and Collins, the Court GRANTS Fairmont's request for a protective order. NMD has not established good cause for taking these depositions, and there appear to be several other more practicable means to obtain the information. Spectra-Physics, Inc. v. Superior Court (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 1487, 1493. With respect to the deposition notices of Mr. Lund and Mr. O'Brien, the Court DENIES Fairmont's request for a protective order as moot, based on the representations in the parties' briefing that: 1) Fairmont has agreed to make Mr. Lund available for deposition; and 2) NMD has withdrawn its deposition notice for Mr. O'Brien. The Court DENIES Fairmont's request for sanctions at this time. The Parties are ordered to appear at the hearing with respect to the outstanding PMK deposition notice, document requests and interrogatories. Prior to the hearing, NMD's counsel shall review its outstanding document requests and interrogatories and be prepared to identify to the Court and discuss the limited requests that seek non-privileged information and are necessary to prepare for trial. Fairmont's counsel shall prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dkruze@mofo.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARY-ANN RICH VS. WILLIAM DOUGLAS BREIDENBACH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 1, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANTS WILLIAM BREIDENBACH AND ARIE AFLALO'S Motion To Set Aside Default. Defendants' motion to set aside default is GRANTED. Public policy favors determining lawsuits on their merits and CCP 473 is to be liberally construed. (Zamora v. Clayborn Contracting Group, Inc. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 249, 255-56.) Defendants are sanctioned $1,000 under CCP 473(c)(1)(A) and also ordered to pay $2,000 of the costs and attorney fees plaintiff incurred in serving process and obtaining default. (See Rogalski v. Nabers Cadillac (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 816, 822.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MARGARET SCHIMMEL ET AL VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 1, 2018, Line 10, PLAINTIFFS MARGARET SCHIMMEL AND JOSEPH SCHIMMEL'S Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiffs' motion to compel responses to special interrogatories is GRANTED. Defendant is to provide meaningful verified responses to interrogatories 3 and 4 within 15 days of a signed order. Plaintiffs' motion for sanctions is DENIED, as it did not identify whom sanctions were sought against. (See CCP 2023.040.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
